factors.summary: Transportation and Financial Services segments are growing fastest.

evidence.text: Transportation revenue for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2017 , compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2016 , continued to experience solid organic recurring and non-recurring growth, led primarily by our automotive product offerings. [...] Financial Services revenue for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2017 , compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2016 , experienced strength across both the recurring and non-recurring revenue base, with some moderation in our Processing product offerings organic revenue growth in the third quarter of 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Transportation and Financial Services segments are growing fastest, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: The significant increases in absolute total cost of revenue and SG&A expense was due to the Merger. As a percentage of revenue, total cost of revenue and SG&A expense declined primarily because of the higher margin Financial Services segment, as well as ongoing cost management and rationalization efforts associated with acquisition integration.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Revenue and SG&A Expense

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, which can improve profitability.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable with slight increases.

evidence.text: Organic revenue increase for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2017 was primarily attributable to strong growth in our Transportation segment (particularly automotive offerings), solid growth in our Financial Services segment, and stabilization in our Resources annual contract value (ACV).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is stable with slight increases, suggesting a favorable market position.

factors.summary: Volume trends are growing, driven by real demand.

evidence.text: Recurring variable revenue was composed entirely of Financial Services revenue, with strong organic growth coming from our Information and Solutions product offering categories within that segment throughout 2017 and from Processing product offerings in the first and second quarters of 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends are growing due to real demand, indicating healthy market conditions.

factors.summary: Demand appears to be stable or slightly increasing across segments.

evidence.text: Adjusted EBITDA increased primarily due to the Merger, as well as continued cost management efforts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is stable or slightly increasing, which is a positive sign for future growth.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to cost management and Merger synergies.

evidence.text: As a percent of revenue, consolidated Adjusted EBITDA margin has increased from realization of Merger cost synergies and margin flow through from revenue growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to cost management and Merger synergies, leading to higher profitability.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Decrease in accrued compensation was primarily due to the 2016 bonus payout made in the first quarter of 2017, partially offset by the current year accrual.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, enhancing efficiency.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by higher operating performance and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: The increase in free cash flow was primarily due to the Merger and increased operating performance, partially offset by increased capital expenditure activity.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by higher operating performance and capital expenditures, indicating strong financial health.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling investment in future growth.

evidence.text: Higher capital expenditures in 2017 compared to 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures indicate investment in future growth, which is a positive sign.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing through share repurchase programs.

evidence.text: We had repurchased approximately $1.571 billion of common shares under the program. We expect to continue repurchasing shares through the remainder of 2017 and into 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns are increasing through share repurchases, showing commitment to returning value to investors.

factors.summary: Acquisition-related costs are being managed, with successful integration efforts.

evidence.text: Acquisition-related costs were $14.9 million in 2017 compared to $104.5 million in 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Reduction in acquisition-related costs suggests effective management and successful integration, which is positive.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is reported as stable with slight increases, though there are indications of acceleration driven by factors such as the Merger and cost management efforts.

evidence.text: Reported organic revenue growth for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2017 , compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2016 , was 5 percent and 4 percent, respectively. After normalizing for the current year Boiler Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) release, organic revenue growth was 4 percent and 3 percent, respectively.
Adjusted EBITDA increased primarily due to the Merger, as well as continued cost management efforts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable organic revenue growth with signs of acceleration indicates a positive trend in demand and performance.
